task_completion.description: Import the custom proxy certificate into Tor Browser.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the Tor Browser Certificate Manager (Authorities tab) with a file selection dialog opening '/home/ga/Documents/ProxyRootCA.crt', or the certificate successfully listed under the Authorities.

task_completion.description: Set the imported proxy certificate to be trusted to identify websites.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the 'Downloading Certificate' or 'Edit Trust Settings' dialog in Tor Browser with the checkbox 'Trust this CA to identify websites.' explicitly checked.

task_completion.description: Export the built-in 'ISRG Root X1' certificate to the specified backup path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing 'ISRG Root X1' selected in the Tor Browser Certificate Manager and a save dialog exporting it to '/home/ga/Documents/ISRG_Root_Backup.crt', or a file manager showing the newly created backup file in that location.

task_completion.description: Configure the minimum TLS version in about:config.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the Tor Browser 'about:config' page with the preference 'security.tls.version.min' searched and its value set to '3'.

task_completion.description: Configure the certificate pinning enforcement level in about:config.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the Tor Browser 'about:config' page with the preference 'security.cert_pinning.enforcement_level' searched and its value set to '2'.

integrity.description: The agent used the Tor Browser GUI to make configuration changes rather than bypassing it via terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the actual Tor Browser interface (Certificate Manager, about:config) being interacted with, rather than terminal commands modifying 'prefs.js' or using 'certutil'.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely exported the certificate from the browser.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the browser's export/save dialog being used for the ISRG Root X1 certificate, rather than a terminal 'cp' command copying a system certificate to the destination.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a text editor to manually write the expected configuration values.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should not show text editors (like nano, vim, or gedit) being used to directly edit Tor Browser's 'prefs.js' file to insert the expected values.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The proxy CA certificate to be imported is located at '/home/ga/Documents/ProxyRootCA.crt'. The ISRG Root X1 certificate must be exported to exactly '/home/ga/Documents/ISRG_Root_Backup.crt'. In about:config, 'security.tls.version.min' must be set to exactly '3' (which corresponds to TLS 1.2), and 'security.cert_pinning.enforcement_level' must be set to exactly '2'.
